FeaturedFeatured

  • PostPost
    It is with a deep sense of gratitude that we announce another pivotal moment in our journey at d-Matrix… a broadly supported (and oversubscribed!) $110 million Series B funding round.

    We took a bet to focus on large model inference at scale and are fortunate to have the market back us – grateful for the participation of a stellar group of new investors in addition to our existing supporters who have believed in our vision from the beginning.

    Of course, none of this would be possible without the talented global d-Matrix team who are delivering on the dream of a compute solution purpose-built for LLMs and making cost-effective, sustainable, scalable hashtag#generativeAI inference a reality.

    This funding further propels our mission to be a leader in efficient AI inference and sets our course for 2024, in which we will bring our highly anticipated Corsair platform to market. This world’s first Digital-In Memory Compute (DIMC), chiplet-based inference platform will redefine the category.

    Thank you to all who have supported, contributed and encouraged us. Looking very forward to what’s to come!
    AI chip startup d-Matrix raises $110 million with backing from Microsoft
    reuters.com • 2 min read
  • PostPost
    AI is on an unsustainable trajectory – there isn’t enough hashtag#compute or energy to meet current or future demand. This is our reason for being.

    After 20+ years in systems leadership – anticipating the mushrooming challenges for efficient hashtag#ArtificialIntelligence inference – Sudeep Bhoja and I took the leap to found d-Matrix. For the past four years, we’ve been building a company that delivers purpose-built hashtag#hardware for efficient hashtag#AI at scale.

    As referenced in this article, companies are shrinking their models to make them more efficient, and while that will help in the short-term, real solutions require new architectures.

    Conversations we’re having across the industry, coverage like this, and customer demands all reinforce that we've got the right solutions in the right places at the right time. Grateful to our growing team working hard to support the future of sustainable AI. https://lnkd.in/gvwcrfMS
    The AI Boom Runs on Chips, but It Can’t Get Enough
    wsj.com • Subscription may be required
  • PostPost
    Companies are putting a huge emphasis on the cost-effective deployment of hashtag#ArtificialIntelligence, but running hashtag#inference can take as much as 2-3X the amount of power needed to train a model.

    Example: It is estimated that for training alone, GPT-3 consumed 1,287 MWh or the equivalent of 550 tons of CO2 emissions (the same amount as a single person taking 550 roundtrips between NYC and San Francisco). This does not even begin to account for the inference to support hashtag#ChatGPT’s now 13M users/day as a standalone product, or powering 500M Bing searches/day, or other players in the hashtag#generativeAI market (source: https://lnkd.in/gWkCSHq7).

    I wrote about the imminent needs, challenges with current processor architectures, and opportunities for new sustainable hashtag#AI approaches for SiliconANGLE & theCUBE: https://lnkd.in/gdmRF8nJ

    hashtag#computing hashtag#sustainability hashtag#MachineLearning hashtag#ML hashtag#datacenters
    Generative AI drives an explosion in compute: The looming need for sustainable AI - SiliconANGLE
    siliconangle.com • 5 min read
  • PostPost
    Congratulations to the d-Matrix team on the launch of Jayhawk, bringing chiplets to hashtag#AI computing with 10-20X performance improvements to customers running generative AI inference and hashtag#LLM transformer applications.

    This is a major milestone as we build the world’s first in-memory hashtag#computing platform with a chiplet-based architecture targeted for power-hungry and latency-sensitive demands of hashtag#GenerativeAI.

    There was no better place to debut this technology than hashtag#ChipletSummit alongside talented colleagues. Special thanks to Wen-Sin Liew, Irene Quek, Ankit Bhargav, Vinayak Patankar, Chris Nicol, Mark Wutzke, Farhad Shakeri, and Cynthia F.

    More on the announcement from Design News: https://lnkd.in/g5EEamgZ
  • PostPost
    Case study that showcases how d-Matrix in partnership with Education for Microsoft Azure and SixNines taped-out the world's first brain-inspired in-memory compute (IMC) chip for datacenter AI entirely in the cloud, all in 14 months. Congrats to team d-Matrix and thanks to the support of our partners.
    World’s First Brain-Inspired AI Inferencing Chip Taped Out on the Azure Cloud
    info.sixninesit.com

Loaded 3 Posts posts
  • Sid Sheth posted this • 1d1d
    Some noteworthy developments over the last few weeks as new models like OpenAI’s Sora and Google’s Gemma open-source family debut and become more capable. These new models are opening up exciting possibilities for new applications. However, the cost of inference and escalating demands for memory and bandwidth continue to pose a very real barrier to the widespread adoption of generative AI.

    We set out to solve this at     d-Matrix so enterprises can take full advantage of fast-evolving model technology using cost efficient solutions purpose-built for inference. Our combination of 2048 digital in-memory compute (DIMC) cores, along with 16GB of SRAM and >1TB of LPDDR in a single node 8-card AI server, enables us to deliver industry-leading throughput and low latency inference for a wide range of models. For instance, with the Llama 70B model, we expect to be able to generate 350 tokens per second with 48 Corsair cards, vastly improving TCO over incumbent solutions, towards our mission of commercially viable generative AI for all.

    Our VP of Product Sree Ganesan discusses the need for more efficient compute options and the potential of IMC technology to solve challenges of generative AI in insideHPC.
    In-Memory Computing Could Be the Inference Breakthrough AI Needs - High-Performance Computing News Analysis | insideHPC
    insidehpc.com • 4 min read
  • Sid Sheth posted this • 1mo1mo
    India is uniquely poised for economic transformation in a global race to realize the opportunity of hashtag#AI. As an India native who continues to conduct business in the region, it was an honor to help with the opening keynote at the India Electronics and Semiconductor Association Vision Summit last week about the country's ambitious vision for AI.

    The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has outlined a clear path forward, including bridging gaps in existing compute infrastructure, funding, governance and workforce skills to accelerate AI innovation and unlock hundreds of billions in GDP.

    Project BHASHINI is a perfect example of this progress, which is paving the way for broader participation in the digital economy by cataloging India’s 1,600+ languages and breaking down the language barrier which prevents so many from accessing content online.

    Thanks to all who took part in the hashtag#IESAVisionSummit2024 and will play a role in making India’s vision a reality.

    PS: d-Matrix is hiring talented individuals in India and beyond. Join us in revolutionizing the world of AI: https://lnkd.in/gXrYE7Kp
  • Sid Sheth reposted this • 1mo1mo
    How will companies create value with hashtag#GenAI in 2024? hashtag#Answer is the era of hashtag#inference. Ready to join a team who is building the future of more efficient, cost-effective hashtag#AI compute.

AboutAbout

Sid Sheth is an experienced entrepreneur, business builder and executive in the semiconductor industry having managed businesses spanning processors and connectivity for cloud, telecom, wireless infrastructure and enterprise. He brings over 20+ years of experience incubating and managing businesses as a start up and as part of a publicly traded company, that have grown to over 100M$ in revenue.

Currently, Sid is Founder & CEO at d-Matrix Corp, a start-up he co-founded in 2019, building an accelerated computing platform for AI inference, with a focus on unparalleled levels of efficiency. The goal of the start-up is to help change the trajectory of efficient AI compute for massively large workloads in the datacenter.

Prior to d-Matrix, as Sr. Vice President & GM for the Networking Business Unit (HSC BU) at Inphi Corporation (NASDAQ : IPHI), he incubated and grew the networking business into a 200M$+ business in 8+ years with a focus on the datacenter segment (cloud, enterprise, 5G), the fastest growing and largest business segment at Inphi. Later, Inphi was acquired by Marvell (NASDAQ:MRVL) in a $10B deal.

Prior to Inphi, Mr. Sheth was at NetLogic Microsystems (now Broadcom Corp.), where he held worldwide marketing responsibility for NetLogic’s enterprise, telecom networking interconnect chips. At NetLogic, he pioneered the company’s 100G product portfolio and led NetLogic’s entry into the mobile and cloud markets eventually helping that business grow to over 100M$

In 2001, Mr. Sheth was a founding team member of Aeluros, a mixed-signal networking IC company, where he held global marketing responsibility, until its successful acquisition by NetLogic in 2007.

Mr. Sheth also spent many years at Intel Corp, where he held engineering and management positions in the Pentium microprocessor and network processor groups.

Mr. Sheth is a regularly featured speaker at industry tradeshows and conferences, is a published author at ISSCC and other technical conferences and has an M.S.E.E from Purdue University.

Executive management, marketing, PNL and sales experience for a profitable product line at a publicly traded company.     d-Matrix · Full-timed-Matrix · Full-time May 2019 - Present · 4 yrs 10 mosMay 2019 - Present · 4 yrs 10 mos United StatesUnited States
      • d-Matrix (https://www.d-matrix.ai/) is building a first-of-its-kind compute platform for AI inference at scale targeting the Generative AI use case. Our goal is to make Generative AI commercially viable for all. A combination of software-first, circuits to systems innovation around in-memory compute and chiplet level interconnects, the team has cracked the code on the final frontier in AI compute efficiency.

        The company has raised $160m+ in funding from Temasek, Playground Global, Microsoft M12 Venture Fund, Industry Ventures, Marlan Holdings, Ericsson, Cortes Capital, Mirae Asset, Samsung, SK Hynix, Nautilus Ventures, Marvell Technology, Entrada Ventures        d-Matrix (https://www.d-matrix.ai/) is building a first-of-its-kind compute platform for AI inference at scale targeting the Generative AI use case.       • Siddharth is one of those rare individuals that can bridge technology and business in an effortless and natural way. Being an accomplished technologist gives him instant credibility with customers, so they take him seriously and are willing to listen to him intently.

        Siddharth has an excellent grasp of marketing techniques and processes. He has demonstrated repeatedly that he can go into a new area and quickly be proficient with the market trends and get to know the people that influence it. He insights and persistence were instrumental in the massive and overwhelming success of the transceiver ICs made by Aeluros, and later by Netlogic.

      • Subra is one of the best verification engineers I have worked with. The speed with which he became a power user for a third party verification tool like SONET workbench at Jasmine Networks, surprised most of his colleagues. He exhibited thoroughness and was diligent as he went about learning the tool. He then used his newly acquired skills to dig up corner cases and marginalities in the design. PublicationsPublications

  • An Integrated VCSEL Driver for 10Gb Ethernet CMOSAn Integrated VCSEL Driver for 10Gb Ethernet CMOS
    IEEE International Solid-State Circuits Conference (ISSCC) 2006 · Feb 7, 2006IEEE International Solid-State Circuits Conference (ISSCC) 2006 · Feb 7, 2006
    • Show publication
      • A 10.3Gb/s VCSEL driver is integrated with a complete Ethernet transceiver in a standard 0.13mum CMOS process. When driving a VCSEL differentially, the resulting optical eye exceeds the 10Gb/s Ethernet mask by 35%. Intended for short-reach applications, the driver dissipates 85mW from 1.2V and occupies 0.15mm2A 10.3Gb/s VCSEL driver is integrated with a complete Ethernet transceiver in a standard 0.13mum CMOS process. When driving a VCSEL differentially, the resulting optical eye exceeds the 10Gb/s Ethernet mask by 35%. Intended for short-reach applications, the driver dissipates 85mW from 1.2V and occupies 0.15mm2
      • Other authorsOther authors
        • 10
  • An 800mW 10-Gigabit Ethernet Transceiver in 0.13um CMOSAn 800mW 10-Gigabit Ethernet Transceiver in 0.13um CMOS
    IEEE International Solid-State Circuits Conference (ISSCC) 2004 · Feb 17, 2004IEEE International Solid-State Circuits Conference (ISSCC) 2004 · Feb 17, 2004
    • Show publication
      • A fully integrated 10 Gb Ethernet transceiver IC using a standard 0.13 μm CMOS process integrates 10.3 Gb/s and 4×3.12 Gb/s analog front-ends, with Layer-1 coding and management functionality. The 2.5×5 mm2 IC exceeds both 10GE and SONET specifications, and dissipates 800 mW from its 1.2/2.5 V supplies.A fully integrated 10 Gb Ethernet transceiver IC using a standard 0.13 μm CMOS process integrates 10.3 Gb/s and 4×3.12 Gb/s analog front-ends, with Layer-1 coding and management functionality. The 2.5×5 mm2 IC exceeds both 10GE and SONET specifications, and dissipates 800 mW from its 1.2/2.5 V supplies.

PatentsPatents

  • Single-chip control module for an integrated system-on-a-chip for silicon photonicsSingle-chip control module for an integrated system-on-a-chip for silicon photonics
    US 10055375 · Issued Aug 21, 2018US 10055375 · Issued Aug 21, 2018
    • See patent
      • The present invention provides an integrated system-on-chip device. The device is configured on a single silicon substrate member. The device has a data input/output interface provided on the substrate member. The device has an input/output block provided on the substrate member and coupled to the data input/output interface. The device has a signal processing block provided on the substrate member and coupled to the input/output block. The device has a driver module provided on the substrate member and coupled to the signal processing block. The device further includes a driver interface and coupled to the driver module and configured to be coupled to a silicon photonics device. In an example, a control block is configured to receive and send instruction(s) in a digital format to the communication block and is configured to receive and send signals in an analog format to communicate with the silicon photonics device.The present invention provides an integrated system-on-chip device. The device is configured on a single silicon substrate member. The device has a data input/output interface provided on the substrate member. The device has an input/output block provided on the substrate member and coupled to the data input/output interface. The device has a signal processing block provided on the substrate member and coupled to the input/output block. The device has a driver module provided on the substrate member and coupled to the signal processing block. The device further includes a driver interface and coupled to the driver module and configured to be coupled to a silicon photonics device. In an example, a control block is configured to receive and send instruction(s) in a digital format to the communication block and is configured to receive and send signals in an analog format to communicate with the silicon photonics device.
      • Other inventorsOther inventors
  • Packet ProcessingPacket Processing
    US US20020064176A1US US20020064176A1
    • See patent
      • A programmable intra-packet switching method includes a port polling process which polls, in a systematic fashion, data ports connected to a network to determine which port, if any, contains a data packet available for processing. A packet fragmentation process fragments the available data packet into at least one data cell having a defined size, wherein this fragmentation continues until a user-defined number of cells are generated.A programmable intra-packet switching method includes a port polling process which polls, in a systematic fashion, data ports connected to a network to determine which port, if any, contains a data packet available for processing. A packet fragmentation process fragments the available data packet into at least one data cell having a defined size, wherein this fragmentation continues until a user-defined number of cells are generated.
